ID:       ddx-424fdac4
Title:    Ratify DDx specs for Fizeau-owned routing and no-agent cleanup
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:docs, area:spec, area:fizeau, kind:design, breaking, decomposed
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-f088a3fd
Deps:     ddx-e2586179, ddx-242d2781, ddx-55559451, ddx-119ddb10, ddx-b42d73be
Desc:     PROBLEM
Implementation agents need durable specs before removing `ddx agent`, renaming DDx internals, or consuming Fizeau contract changes. Today the specs still mix the old `agent` vocabulary with the desired DDx/Fizeau boundary, so agents can reasonably follow stale instructions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 still describes an `Agent Passthrough` structural mount for `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still describes the upstream invocation boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` and `DDx Agent Service` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:1-23. FEAT-010 still says layer 1 consumes upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. TP-020 remains a historical DDx-side routing test plan even though Fizeau owns routing. docs/migrations/routing-config.md:1-13 removes some old routing keys but does not yet document the full `agent.*` removal / Fizeau-vs-execution config split.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the specs before implementation. Required docs: FEAT-001, FEAT-006 or its renamed replacement, FEAT-010, ADR-024, TP-020, docs/migrations/routing-config.md, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md, and DDx skill references. The specs must state: DDx owns task execution (`run`, `try`, `work`); Fizeau owns routing, provider/model discovery, native transcript/session rendering, and fuzzy model constraint resolution; `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are explicit operator passthrough only; DDx passes raw strings unchanged; `ddx agent` is removed without deprecation; and `agent` is allowed only for actual external AI agent concepts.

NON-SCOPE
No production code removal in this bead. No Fizeau implementation work. No compatibility alias design. No generated CLI docs rebuild unless the docs generator is part of the spec-doc workflow.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz` contract identity
- fizeau-400b57fe for Fizeau-owned model alias/fuzzy matching

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-f088a3fd. Dependencies: none; this bead comes first.
Accept:   1. FEAT-001 removes the `ddx agent` structural mount and defines only `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and any Fizeau diagnostics namespace that remains, with no aliases.
2. FEAT-006 is renamed or rewritten as the DDx consumer spec for Fizeau, and no longer presents `agentlib.DdxAgent` / `ddx-agent` as the desired contract name.
3. FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.
4. ADR-024 says escalation changes only MinPower/MaxPower/request facts and Fizeau chooses the concrete route.
5. TP-020 is replaced or superseded with a DDx boundary test plan proving DDx does not normalize, fuzzy-match, or route models.
6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md documents removal of `agent.*` defaults and the split between Fizeau routing config and DDx execution config.
7. DDx skill references explain explicit passthrough and Fizeau-owned model matching using examples such as `--model qwen36`, while stating DDx passes the raw string unchanged.
8. `rg -n "ddx agent|ddx-agent|agentlib.DdxAgent|Agent Service|cli/internal/agent" docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited historical or literal-external-agent hits documented in the change.
9. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

ID:       ddx-ddacd4ff
Title:    C14: delete ExecuteBead* shims and aliases; verify all callers ported
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:cleanup
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-387a0178, ddx-3ee4ddcf
Desc:     PROBLEM
After the C13 refactor (ddx-387a0178), shim typedefs of the form ExecuteBeadWorker, ExecuteBeadReport, ExecuteBeadResult, ExecuteBeadStatus, ExecuteBeadOutcome remain in the codebase as temporary aliases. These shims prevent callers from adopting the canonical names and leave dead identifier surface that grep/tooling cannot distinguish from active usage.

ROOT CAUSE
C13 (ddx-387a0178) introduces the canonical type names as part of the file/identifier rename step and adds shim typedefs for backward compat during the migration window. The shim typedefs live in the files renamed by C13 (exact file:line determined after C13 lands). The CLI aliases ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead continue to register live cobra commands after C13, pointing at logic now accessible via ddx work and ddx try.

PROPOSED FIX
- Sweep all callsites off the shim names (grep for ExecuteBeadWorker | ExecuteBeadReport | ExecuteBeadResult | ExecuteBead\b | ExecuteBeadStatus | ExecuteBeadOutcome in cli/).
- Delete the shim typedefs from C13.
- Remove cobra command registrations for ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead; replace with a cobra RunE that prints a deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
- Verify: grep returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test files.

NON-SCOPE
- Deleting the entire ddx agent command group (that is ddx-d8474e0e, a separate bead).
- Changing execute-loop/try behavior.
Accept:   1. grep -rn 'ExecuteBeadWorker\|ExecuteBeadReport\|ExecuteBeadResult\|ExecuteBead\b\|ExecuteBeadStatus\|ExecuteBeadOutcome' cli/ returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test source files.
2. ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead cobra commands replaced with deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
3. TestNoBuildRegressions_AfterShimDelete: cd cli && go build ./... green.
4. cd cli && go test ./... green.
5. Server worker integration test green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
7. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.RecoverOrphans (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:383) is invoked from the ddx try / ddx work code path. Today it is called ONLY from cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:168 across the entire CLI tree (verified by grep). If it is not yet wired into the try/work path, lift the call into ExecuteBeadWithConfig (engine) or into try.Attempt before this bead's cobra-replacement step. A regression test (TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn, in cli/cmd/try_test.go) must assert the behavior survives.
8. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.BuildLandingGateContext (cli/internal/agent/landing_gate_context.go:28) is invoked when landing through the ddx try CLI path. Today it is called from cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:206 and from cli/internal/server/workers.go:1700 (server-side worker), but NOT from the CLI try/work path (verified by grep). If not wired through, lift into the landing helper used by try.Attempt. A regression test (TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding, in cli/cmd/try_test.go) must assert the behavior survives.
9. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService preflight (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:390, called at cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:160-164 when no AgentRunnerOverride) is either invoked from ddx try / ddx work (CLI) or explicitly documented as N/A in this bead's notes with rationale. Today try.go does not call it.
10. INTERSECTIONS: ddx-d8474e0e (full ddx agent retirement) reuses the same engine wiring; the lifts in AC #7-9 are prerequisites for both this bead and ddx-d8474e0e. ddx-a1ae37ff (commit-scoping --no-verify fix) is independent and lands first.

1	package cmd

21	)

23	// Exit codes for ddx try terminal outcomes. These are specific to ddx try
24	// and intentionally do not reuse the generic ExitCode* constants because
25	// the bead-execution outcome vocabulary is distinct from generic CLI errors.
26	const (
27		tryExitSuccess         = 0 // success or already_satisfied
28		tryExitPreservedOrNone = 1 // preserved_for_review or no_changes
29		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
30	)
31	
32	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
33	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
34	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
35	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
36	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
37		cmd := &cobra.Command{
38			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
39			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
40			SilenceUsage: true,
41			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
42	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
43	
44	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
45	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
46	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
47	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
48	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
49	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
50	
51	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
52	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
53	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
54	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
55	
56	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
57	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
58	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
59	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
60	
61	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
62	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
63	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
64	
65	Exit codes:
66	  0  success or already_satisfied
67	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
68	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
69	`,
70			Example: `  # Execute a specific bead
71	  ddx try ddx-abc12345
72	
73	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
74	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini
75	
76	  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
77	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing`,
78			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
79			RunE: f.runTry,
80		}
81	
82		// Routing knobs — identical to "ddx work" passthrough surface.
83		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
84		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
85		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
86		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
87		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
89		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
90		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
91		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
92		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
93		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
94		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
95		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
98	
99		return cmd
100	}
101	
102	// runTry is the RunE for "ddx try <bead-id>".
103	func (f *CommandFactory) runTry(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
104		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
105			return err
106		}
107	
108		beadID := args[0]
109	
110		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
111		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
112	
113		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
114		// Runs before claim/worktree setup so the operator sees the warning before
115		// any bead state changes.
116		preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
117		f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
118			emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
119		})
120	
121		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
122		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
123		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
124		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
125		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
126		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
127		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
128		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
129		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
130		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
131		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
132		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
133		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
134		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
135	
136		if noReview && !noReviewAck {
137			return fmt.Errorf("--no-review requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing (break-glass acknowledgement)")
138		}
139	
140		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
141	
142		// Pre-flight: look up the bead.
143		target, err := store.Get(beadID)
144		if err != nil {
145			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead not found: %s\n", beadID)
146			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
147		}
148	
149		// Pre-flight: verify the bead is in a claimable state.
150		// Claimable = open, or stale in_progress (the loop handles stale reclaim).
151		switch target.Status {
152		case bead.StatusOpen, bead.StatusInProgress:
153			// allowed — loop's Claim() handles stale-heartbeat reclaim
154		default:
155			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not claimable (status=%s)\n", target.Status)
156			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
157		}
158	
159		// Pre-flight: check for unmet dependencies.
160		if unmet := unmetDeps(store, target); len(unmet) > 0 {
161			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead has unmet dependencies: %s\n", strings.Join(unmet, ", "))
162			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
163		}
164	
165		// Wrap the real store so that ReadyExecution() returns only the target bead.
166		// All other operations pass through to the underlying store unchanged.
167		forcedStore := &singleBeadStore{Store: store, target: *target}
168	
169		// Set up progress logging (same as runAgentExecuteLoopImpl).
170		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
171		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
172		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
173		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
174		var loopSink io.Writer
175		if lf, openErr := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); openErr == nil {
176			loopSink = lf
177			defer lf.Close() //nolint:errcheck
178		}
179	
180		// Worker-probe (best-effort server mirror; non-blocking).
181		probeIdent := workerprobe.Identity{
182			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
183			Harness:      harness,
184			Model:        model,
185			ExecutorPID:  os.Getpid(),
186			ExecutorHost: hostnameOrEmpty(),
187			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
188		}
189		probe := workerprobe.New(probeIdent, workerprobe.Config{
190			AddrFunc: serverpkg.ReadServerAddr,
191		})
192		probeCtx, probeCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
193		probe.Start(probeCtx)
194		defer func() {
195			probeCancel()
196			probe.Stop()
197		}()
198		if loopSink != nil {
199			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(loopSink, probe)
200		} else {
201			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(io.Discard, probe)
202		}
203	
204		// Process-local LandCoordinator.
205		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
206		defer localCoord.Stop()
207	
208		// Post-merge reviewer (on by default, skipped via --no-review).
209		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
210		if !noReview {
211			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
212				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
213				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
214				BeadEvents:  bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
215				Harness:     reviewHarness,
216				Model:       reviewModel,
217			}
218		}
219	
220		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
221	
222		// Determine whether zero-config auto-route applies (same logic as runWork).
223		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
224			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
225		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
226		resourceChecker := buildCLIResourceChecker(projectRoot, f.resourceCheckerOverride)
227	
228		// Build the executor: either the test override or the real single-tier executor.
229		var executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
230		if f.tryExecutorOverride != nil {

267				}

269					return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
270				}
271				if execErr != nil {
272					agent.MarkResultExecutionError(res, execErr)
273					return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
274				}
275				if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
276					targetBead, _ := store.Get(execBeadID)
277					landRes, _, landErr := agent.SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
278						ctx, projectRoot, targetBead, res,
279						func(req agent.LandRequest) (*agent.LandResult, error) { return localCoord.Submit(req) },

281						resolveClaimAssignee(), "ddx try",

297					}

312			Store:    forcedStore,

314			Reviewer: reviewer,

318			Assignee: resolveClaimAssignee(),
319			Harness:  harness,
320			Model:    model,
321			Provider: provider,
322			ModelRef: modelRef,
323			Profile:  profile,
324			Effort:   effort,
325		}

329		}

331			if resErr, ok := err.(*agent.ResourceExhaustedError); ok && resErr != nil {
332				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), resErr.Error())
333			}
334			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: err.Error()}
335		}
336	
337		cliLandingOps := agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
338		var qualityRunner agent.AgentRunner
339		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
340			qualityRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
341		}
342		lintHook := agent.NewPreDispatchLintHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner)
343		triageHook := agent.NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner, nil)
344		result, runErr := worker.Run(cmd.Context(), rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
345			Mode:                  executeloop.ModeOnce,
346			Log:                   cmd.OutOrStdout(),
347			EventSink:             loopSink,
348			WorkerID:              resolveClaimAssignee(),
349			ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
350			ResourceChecker:       resourceChecker,
351			SessionID:             loopSessionID,

353			PreDispatchLintHook:   lintHook,
354			PostAttemptTriageHook: triageHook,
355			NoReview:              noReview,
356		})

358			if (errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(runErr, context.DeadlineExceeded)) && result != nil && len(result.Results) > 0 {
359				writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.Results[0])
360			}
361			return runErr
362		}

364		// Report outcome.

367			// This can happen if a heartbeat re-appeared just before claim, making it
368			// not stale-enough for reclaim. Surface a clear message.
369			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not execution-ready (it may be actively claimed by another worker): %s\n", beadID)
370			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
371		}
372	
373		if len(result.Results) == 0 {
374			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
375		}
376		report := result.Results[0]
377		writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), report)
378		if intent := escalation.ParseExecutionHint(target); intent.Source == escalation.ExecutionIntentSourceBeadHint && intent.RequestedTier != "" {
379			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "routing intent: tier=%s source=%s\n", intent.RequestedTier, intent.Source)
380		}
381	
382		return tryExitCodeForStatus(report.Status)
383	}
384	
385	func writeTryResult(w io.Writer, report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
386		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nbead: %s\nstatus: %s\n", report.BeadID, report.Status)
387		if report.Detail != "" {
388			fmt.Fprintf(w, "detail: %s\n", report.Detail)
389		}
390		if report.ResultRev != "" {
391			fmt.Fprintf(w, "result_rev: %s\n", report.ResultRev)
392		}
393		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
394			fmt.Fprintf(w, "preserve_ref: %s\n", report.PreserveRef)
395		}
396	}
397	
398	// tryExitCodeForStatus maps a bead execution status string to the ddx try exit code contract.
399	func tryExitCodeForStatus(status string) error {
400		switch status {
401		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
402			return nil // exit 0
403		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
404			agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced:
405			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitPreservedOrNone, Message: ""}
406		default:
407			// execution_failed and all other terminal non-success statuses
408			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
409		}
410	}
411	
412	// unmetDeps returns the IDs of deps that are not yet closed, in the order
413	// they appear in the bead's dependency list.
414	func unmetDeps(store *bead.Store, b *bead.Bead) []string {
415		depIDs := b.DepIDs()
416		if len(depIDs) == 0 {
417			return nil
418		}
419		var unmet []string
420		for _, depID := range depIDs {
421			dep, err := store.Get(depID)
422			if err != nil || dep.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
423				unmet = append(unmet, depID)
424			}
425		}
426		return unmet
427	}
428	
429	// singleBeadStore wraps a *bead.Store and overrides ReadyExecution to return
430	// only a single target bead. All other operations are delegated unchanged.
431	// This allows the execute-bead worker to treat the forced bead as if it were
432	// the only entry in the execution-ready queue.
433	type singleBeadStore struct {
434		*bead.Store
435		target bead.Bead
436	}
437	
438	func (s *singleBeadStore) ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error) {
439		return []bead.Bead{s.target}, nil
440	}

141	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
142	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
143	// triage:needs-investigation park.
144	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
145	
146	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
147	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
148	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
149	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
150	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
151	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
152	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
153	//
154	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
155	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
156	//
157	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
158	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
159	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
160	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
161	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
162	// directly.
163	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
164		ctx context.Context,
165		projectRoot string,
166		b *bead.Bead,
167		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
168		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
169		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
170		actor, source string,
171		now func() time.Time,
172	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
173		if now == nil {
174			now = time.Now
175		}
176		if submit == nil {
177			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
178		}
179		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
180		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
181		if err != nil {
182			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
183			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
184			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
185			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
186			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
187				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
188			}
189			return &LandResult{
190				Status:      "preserved",
191				PreserveRef: ref,
192				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
193			}, nil, nil
194		}
195		if outcome.Blocked {
196			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
197			if perr != nil {
198				return nil, outcome, perr
199			}
200			if eventStore != nil {
201				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
202			}
203			return land, outcome, nil
204		}
205		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
206			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
207		}
208		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
209		return land, outcome, sErr
210	}

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
91		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
92		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
93			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
94		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
95		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98	
99		// Register "ddx work plan" and "ddx work focus" as subcommands of "ddx work".
100		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
101		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
102	
103		return cmd
104	}
105	
106	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
107	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
108	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
109	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
110	// by this layer.
111	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
113			return err
114		}
115	
116		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
120		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
121		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123		if !asJSON {
124			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
125			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
126				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
127			})
128		}
129	
130		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
131	}

27	Execute call. It is the layer-1 primitive over which ddx try and ddx work add
28	bead lifecycle, queue semantics, and retry policy.

30	DDx passes all routing knobs (--harness, --provider, --model, --model-ref,

32	or pre-resolution. The service owns route selection within those bounds
33	(CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010).

12	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecutionsCommand() *cobra.Command {
13		cmd := &cobra.Command{
14			Use:   "executions",
15			Short: "Manage execute-bead execution bundles (.ddx/executions/)",
16			Long: `Inspect and retrieve execute-bead execution bundles.
17	
18	Bundles are written by execute-bead under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/. When
19	an out-of-band mirror is configured under executions.mirror in .ddx/config.yaml,
20	each finalized bundle is uploaded to the mirror and indexed in
21	.ddx/executions/mirror-index.jsonl.
22	
23	Use 'ddx agent executions fetch <attempt-id>' to pull a previously mirrored
24	bundle back to local disk.`,
25			Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
26				_ = cmd.Help()
27			},
28		}
29		cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentExecutionsFetchCommand())
30		return cmd
31	}
32	
33	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecutionsFetchCommand() *cobra.Command {
34		cmd := &cobra.Command{
35			Use:   "fetch <attempt-id>",
36			Short: "Pull a mirrored execution bundle back to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/",
37			Long: `fetch retrieves a previously mirrored bundle from the configured execution
38	mirror and writes it back to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ for local inspection.
39	
40	The mirror entry is resolved from .ddx/executions/mirror-index.jsonl. The
41	bundle is rehydrated under the project's .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ path
42	unless --dest is given.`,
43			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
44			RunE: f.runAgentExecutionsFetch,
45		}
46		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
47		cmd.Flags().String("dest", "", "Destination directory (default: .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/)")
48		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output result as JSON")
49		return cmd
50	}
51	
52	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecutionsFetch(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
53		attemptID := args[0]
54		if !validBeadID.MatchString(attemptID) {
55			return fmt.Errorf("invalid attempt id %q", attemptID)
56		}
57		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
58		dest, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("dest")
59		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
60	
61		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
62	
63		entry, err := agent.LookupMirrorEntry(projectRoot, attemptID)
64		if err != nil {
65			return fmt.Errorf("reading mirror index: %w", err)
66		}
67		if entry == nil {
68			return fmt.Errorf("no mirror entry for attempt %s in %s", attemptID, agent.ExecutionsMirrorIndexFile)
69		}
70	
71		backend, err := agent.NewMirrorBackend(entry.Kind)
72		if err != nil {
73			return err
74		}
75	
76		if dest == "" {
77			dest = filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
78		}
79	
80		if err := backend.Fetch(entry.MirrorURI, dest); err != nil {
81			return fmt.Errorf("fetching bundle: %w", err)
82		}
83	
84		if asJSON {
85			out := struct {
86				AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id"`
87				BeadID    string `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
88				MirrorURI string `json:"mirror_uri"`
89				Dest      string `json:"dest"`
90				ByteSize  int64  `json:"byte_size"`
91			}{
92				AttemptID: entry.AttemptID,
93				BeadID:    entry.BeadID,
94				MirrorURI: entry.MirrorURI,
95				Dest:      dest,
96				ByteSize:  entry.ByteSize,
97			}
98			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
99			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
100			return enc.Encode(out)

1495	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteLoopCommand() *cobra.Command {
1496		cmd := &cobra.Command{
1497			Use:   "execute-loop",
1498			Short: "Drain the single-project execution-ready bead queue",
1499			Long: `execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface. It scans the
1500	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
1501	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
1502	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.
1503	
1504	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
1505	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
1506	
1507	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
1508	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.
1509	
1510	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
1511	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
1512	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
1513	  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
1514	  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
1515	  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
1516	  execution_failed             — unclaim
1517	  structural_validation_failed — unclaim
1518	
1519	Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
1520	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
1521	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
1522	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1523	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.
1524	
1525	By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
1526	worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
1527	process.
1528	
1529	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
1530	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
1531	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
1532	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
1533	
1534	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1535	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1536	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1537	`,
1538			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
1539	  ddx agent execute-loop
1540	
1541	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
1542	  ddx agent execute-loop --profile default --once
1543	
1544	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker

=== work.go content to verify ===
package cmd

import (
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
	"github.com/spf13/pflag"
)

// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command as a UX alias for
// "ddx agent execute-loop". All flags and behavior are identical.
func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
	inner := f.newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()

	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "work",
		Short: "Work the bead execution queue",
		Long: `work is the primary operator-facing surface for draining the bead
execution queue. It is an alias for "ddx agent execute-loop" — all flags
and behavior are identical.

` + inner.Long,
		Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
  ddx work

  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
  ddx work --profile default --once

  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
  ddx work --poll-interval 30s

  # Force a specific harness/model for a debugging pass
  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7

  # Run inline in the current process
  ddx work --local --once`,
		Args: inner.Args,
		RunE: inner.RunE,
	}

	// Clone all flags from the execute-loop command
	inner.Flags().VisitAll(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
		cmd.Flags().AddFlag(flag)
	})

	return cmd
}

## Final Report: Agent Command Dependencies in DDx

### 1. Functions from agent_cmd.go Referenced by Non-Agent Files

**From work.go:**
- Line 11: `f.newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()` - called from `newWorkCommand()`

**From command_factory.go:**
- Line 533: `f.newAgentCommand()` - added to root command
- Line 545: `f.newWorkCommand()` - added to root command (this itself calls `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()`)

**Summary:** Only ONE function from agent_cmd.go is directly used by non-agent files:
- `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()` (used by work.go)

### 2. Test Files Referencing Agent Command Constructors

The test files **do NOT** directly call `newAgentCommand()`, `newAgentExecuteBeadCommand()`, or `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()` constructors. Instead, they use the factory pattern:
- Tests call `NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()` and then use `root.Find()` or `executeCommand()` to access commands by their command line path.

**Test files that test agent functionality:**
- `agent_capabilities_test.go`
- `agent_execute_bead_test.go` (47 KB)
- `agent_execute_loop_test.go` (73 lines)
- `agent_metrics_test.go`
- `agent_run_profile_test.go`
- `agent_workers_stop_test.go`
- `work_test.go` (validates work command flags match execute-loop)

### 3. agent_execute_loop_test.go

**Yes, it exists.** 73 lines total. It contains:
- `TestAgentExecuteLoopUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan()` - tests loop uses correct project root
- `TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings()` - tests bead execution integration

Note: No standalone `agent_execute_loop.go` file exists; the `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()` is defined entirely in agent_cmd.go (2321 lines).

### 4. root.go References from agent_cmd.go

**root.go does NOT directly reference agent_cmd.go.** Instead:
- `root.go` defines the `Execute()` function that creates a `NewCommandFactory()` and calls `factory.NewRootCommand()`
- `command_factory.go` (not root.go) adds `f.newAgentCommand()` to the root at line 533

### 5. Functions in agent_executions.go

- `newAgentExecutionsCommand()` - main command
- `newAgentExecutionsFetchCommand()` - subcommand
- `runAgentExecutionsFetch()` - implementation of fetch logic

### 6. agent_execute_loop_escalation.go

**Does NOT exist.** No file with this name found in the repository.

---

## Refactoring Recommendations

### NEEDS TO MOVE (Keep in agent_cmd.go or create agent_execute_loop.go):
1. **`newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()`** - Must stay accessible to work.go
   - work.go directly depends on this (line 11)
   - 45+ lines of code

### CAN BE EXTRACTED/REORGANIZED:
1. **agent_execute_bead.go** - Already separate, self-contained ✓
   - Only called by agent_cmd.go to add subcommand
   - Can remain as-is or stay with agent_cmd.go

2. **agent_executions.go** - Already separate, self-contained ✓
   - Only called by agent_cmd.go to add subcommand
   - Can remain as-is or stay with agent_cmd.go

### ALREADY CORRECT:
1. **work.go** - Properly structured as thin wrapper
   - Depends only on `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()` from agent_cmd.go
   - No circular dependencies

### CLEANUP OPPORTUNITIES:
- The 11 command constructors in agent_cmd.go (2321 lines) could be split by domain:
  - Run/Condense commands → agent_cmd.go (core)
  - List/Capabilities/Doctor/Log/Benchmark/Catalog/Replay → could move to agent_metadata.go or agent_info.go
  - Execute-loop/Execute-bead → keep together or in execute-specific files
  - But this is optional since they're all internal factory methods (not exported)

**Bottom line:** `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()` is the only **critical dependency** that work.go has on agent_cmd.go. Everything else is internal command routing through the CommandFactory pattern.

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
5	)
6	
7	// reviewRepairAttemptArgs bundles the arguments that must be forwarded to
8	// singleTierAttempt for a review_fixable_gap repair cycle. MinPower is raised
9	// above the implementer's actual power; all other routing pins are forwarded
10	// unchanged from the original attempt.
11	type reviewRepairAttemptArgs struct {
12		MinPower int
13		Harness  string
14		Model    string
15		Provider string
16		Profile  string
17		// MaxPower is the operator's explicit cap, preserved as-is (0 = unset).
18		MaxPower int
19	}
20	
21	// reviewFixableGapRepairMinPower returns the MinPower floor for a repair
22	// attempt that follows a review_fixable_gap verdict. It advances the
23	// escalation ladder from the implementer's actual power to the next higher
24	// floor so the repair runs on a stronger model than the original implementer.
25	//
26	// If the ladder has no higher floor (exhausted), it returns implActualPower
27	// so the repair attempt at least carries the review rationale as additional
28	// context even if the same power tier is used.
29	func reviewFixableGapRepairMinPower(repairCtx *agent.RepairContextFromReviewGroup, ladder escalationFloorFinder) int {
30		if repairCtx == nil {
31			return 0
32		}
33		next, err := nextEscalationFloor(ladder, repairCtx.ImplementerActualPower)
34		if err != nil {
35			return repairCtx.ImplementerActualPower
36		}
37		return next
38	}
39	
40	// reviewFixableGapRepairArgs computes the singleTierAttempt arguments for a
41	// repair attempt. MinPower is raised above the implementer's actual power
42	// using the escalation ladder. All explicit harness/model/provider/profile
43	// pins are forwarded unchanged. MaxPower is preserved from the operator's
44	// original setting (0 means unset — the repair must not add a cap the
45	// operator did not request).
46	func reviewFixableGapRepairArgs(
47		repairCtx *agent.RepairContextFromReviewGroup,
48		harness, model, provider, profile string,
49		maxPower int,
50		ladder escalationFloorFinder,
51	) reviewRepairAttemptArgs {
52		return reviewRepairAttemptArgs{
53			MinPower: reviewFixableGapRepairMinPower(repairCtx, ladder),
54			Harness:  harness,
55			Model:    model,
56			Provider: provider,
57			Profile:  profile,
58			MaxPower: maxPower,
59		}
60	}
61

func TestWorkUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)

	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(subdir, 0o755))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(subdir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	var res struct {
		ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
		NoReadyWork bool   `json:"no_ready_work"`

}

15	func appendMetricsRoutingReviewEvents(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID, verdict string, createdAt time.Time) {
16		t.Helper()
17		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
18			"resolved_provider": "claude",
19			"resolved_model":    "sonnet",
20			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
21		})
22		require.NoError(t, err)
23		require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
24			Kind:      "routing",
25			Summary:   "provider=claude model=sonnet",
26			Body:      string(body),
27			Actor:     "ddx",
28			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
29			CreatedAt: createdAt,
30		}))
31		require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
32			Kind:      "review",
33			Summary:   verdict,
34			Body:      "fixture review verdict",
35			Actor:     "worker",
36			Source:    "ddx agent review",
37			CreatedAt: createdAt.Add(time.Second),
38		}))
39	}
40	
41	// TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier verifies the review-outcomes
42	// attribution pipeline over explicit bead evidence: when a kind:routing event
43	// precedes an APPROVE kind:review event, computeReviewOutcomes attributes the
44	// review to that provider/model tier with approvals=1.
45	func TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier(t *testing.T) {
46		dir := t.TempDir()
47		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
48		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
49	
50		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
51		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
52		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-rev-approve", Title: "approve target"}))
53		appendMetricsRoutingReviewEvents(t, store, "ddx-rev-approve", "APPROVE", time.Date(2026, 5, 9, 1, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))
54	
55		events, err := store.Events("ddx-rev-approve")
56		require.NoError(t, err)
57	
58		routingIdx, reviewIdx := -1, -1
59		for i, e := range events {
60			switch e.Kind {
61			case "routing":
62				if routingIdx == -1 {
63					routingIdx = i
64				}
65				var body map[string]any
66				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(e.Body), &body))
67				assert.Equal(t, "claude", body["resolved_provider"])
68				assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", body["resolved_model"])
69			case "review":
70				reviewIdx = i
71				assert.Equal(t, "APPROVE", e.Summary)
72			}
73		}
74		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, routingIdx, 0, "fixture must append a kind:routing event before review")
75		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, reviewIdx, 0, "fixture must append a kind:review event after APPROVE")
76		assert.Less(t, routingIdx, reviewIdx, "routing must precede review for correct tier attribution")
77	
78		report, err := computeReviewOutcomesReport(dir, 0, time.Time{})
79		rows := report.Rows
80		require.NoError(t, err)
81		require.Len(t, rows, 1)
82		assert.Equal(t, "claude/sonnet", rows[0].Tier)
83		assert.Equal(t, "claude", rows[0].Harness)
84		assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", rows[0].Model)
85		assert.Equal(t, 1, rows[0].Reviews)
86		assert.Equal(t, 1, rows[0].Approvals)
87		assert.Equal(t, 0, rows[0].Rejections)
88		assert.InDelta(t, 1.0, rows[0].ApprovalRate, 0.0001)
89	}
90	
91	// TestReviewEvidenceRequestChangesCountedAsRejection verifies that a
92	// REQUEST_CHANGES verdict is attributed to the most recent routing tier and
93	// counted as a rejection in review-outcomes.
94	func TestReviewEvidenceRequestChangesCountedAsRejection(t *testing.T) {
95		dir := t.TempDir()
96		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
97		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
98	
99		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
100		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
101		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-rev-reject", Title: "reject target"}))
102		appendMetricsRoutingReviewEvents(t, store, "ddx-rev-reject", "REQUEST_CHANGES", time.Date(2026, 5, 9, 2, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))
103	
104		events, err := store.Events("ddx-rev-reject")
105		require.NoError(t, err)
106		var hasRouting, hasReview bool
107		for _, e := range events {
108			if e.Kind == "routing" {
109				hasRouting = true
110				var body map[string]any
111				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(e.Body), &body))
112				assert.Equal(t, "claude", body["resolved_provider"])
113				assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", body["resolved_model"])
114			}
115			if e.Kind == "review" {
116				hasReview = true
117				assert.Equal(t, "REQUEST_CHANGES", e.Summary)
118			}
119		}
120		assert.True(t, hasRouting, "fixture must append kind:routing for tier attribution")
121		assert.True(t, hasReview, "fixture must append kind:review with REQUEST_CHANGES summary")
122	
123		report, err := computeReviewOutcomesReport(dir, 0, time.Time{})
124		rows := report.Rows
125		require.NoError(t, err)
126		require.Len(t, rows, 1)
127		assert.Equal(t, "claude/sonnet", rows[0].Tier)
128		assert.Equal(t, 1, rows[0].Reviews)
129		assert.Equal(t, 0, rows[0].Approvals)
130		assert.Equal(t, 1, rows[0].Rejections)
131		assert.InDelta(t, 0.0, rows[0].ApprovalRate, 0.0001)
132	}

14	func TestBeadMetrics(t *testing.T) {
15		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
16		t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_DIR", "")
17	
18		dir := t.TempDir()
19		execRoot := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "executions")
20	
21		// ddx-1: 2 attempts (one succeeded, one failed). Total tokens 3000, cost 1.50.
22		writeExecResult(t, execRoot, "20260401T100000-aaaa0001", map[string]any{
23			"bead_id":     "ddx-1",
24			"harness":     "claude",
25			"model":       "sonnet",
26			"outcome":     "task_succeeded",
27			"duration_ms": 100000,
28			"tokens":      1000,
29			"cost_usd":    0.5,
30		})
31		writeExecResult(t, execRoot, "20260401T110000-aaaa0002", map[string]any{
32			"bead_id":     "ddx-1",
33			"harness":     "claude",
34			"model":       "sonnet",
35			"outcome":     "task_failed",
36			"duration_ms": 200000,
37			"tokens":      2000,
38			"cost_usd":    1.0,
39		})
40		// ddx-2: 1 attempt, succeeded. 500 tokens, 0.25 cost.
41		writeExecResult(t, execRoot, "20260401T120000-aaaa0003", map[string]any{
42			"bead_id":     "ddx-2",
43			"harness":     "claude",
44			"model":       "opus",
45			"outcome":     "task_succeeded",
46			"duration_ms": 300000,
47			"tokens":      500,
48			"cost_usd":    0.25,
49		})
50		// Missing bead_id — should be skipped.
51		writeExecResult(t, execRoot, "20260401T130000-aaaa0004", map[string]any{
52			"harness":     "agent",
53			"outcome":     "error",
54			"duration_ms": 1000,
55		})
56		// Malformed result.json — should be skipped.
57		badDir := filepath.Join(execRoot, "20260401T140000-aaaa0005")
58		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(badDir, 0o755))
59		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(badDir, "result.json"), []byte("not json"), 0o644))
60	
61		factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
62		rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
63	
64		// Seed bead tracker so metrics can render titles.
65		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Fix auth bug", "--priority", "1")
66		require.NoError(t, err)
67		// Override ID via update by recreating — simpler: find generated ID and ignore.
68		// For this test we create beads with explicit IDs via direct store write.
69		// Use bead create + update to set custom IDs is not supported, so write beads.jsonl directly.
70	
71		// Direct seed of beads.jsonl with the IDs used above.
72		beadsPath := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")
73		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(beadsPath, []byte(
74			`{"id":"ddx-1","title":"First bead","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-01T00:00:00Z"}`+"\n"+
75				`{"id":"ddx-2","title":"Second bead","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-01T00:00:00Z"}`+"\n"),
76			0o644))
77	
78		// --json output
79		jsonCmd := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
80		output, err := executeCommand(jsonCmd, "bead", "metrics", "--json")
81		require.NoError(t, err)
82	
83		var rows []beadMetricsRow
84		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(output), &rows))
85		require.Len(t, rows, 2)
86	
87		byID := map[string]beadMetricsRow{}
88		for _, r := range rows {
89			byID[r.BeadID] = r
90		}
91	
92		require.Contains(t, byID, "ddx-1")
93		assert.Equal(t, 2, byID["ddx-1"].AttemptCount)
94		assert.Equal(t, 3000, byID["ddx-1"].TotalTokens)
95		assert.InDelta(t, 1.5, byID["ddx-1"].TotalCostUSD, 0.0001)
96		assert.InDelta(t, 150000.0, byID["ddx-1"].AvgDurationMS, 0.0001)
97		assert.Equal(t, "First bead", byID["ddx-1"].Title)
98	
99		require.Contains(t, byID, "ddx-2")
100		assert.Equal(t, 1, byID["ddx-2"].AttemptCount)
101		assert.Equal(t, 500, byID["ddx-2"].TotalTokens)
102		assert.InDelta(t, 0.25, byID["ddx-2"].TotalCostUSD, 0.0001)
103		assert.Equal(t, "Second bead", byID["ddx-2"].Title)
104	
105		// Table output shape: has header columns.
106		tableCmd := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
107		tableOut, err := executeCommand(tableCmd, "bead", "metrics")
108		require.NoError(t, err)
109		header := strings.SplitN(tableOut, "\n", 2)[0]
110		for _, col := range []string{"BEAD_ID", "ATTEMPTS", "TOTAL_TOKENS", "TOTAL_COST_USD", "AVG_DURATION_MS", "TITLE"} {
111			assert.Contains(t, header, col)
112		}
113	
114		// Empty executions dir returns an empty list, not an error.
115		empty := t.TempDir()
116		emptyCmd := NewCommandFactory(empty).NewRootCommand()
117		emptyOut, err := executeCommand(emptyCmd, "bead", "metrics", "--json")
118		require.NoError(t, err)
119		var emptyRows []beadMetricsRow
120		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(emptyOut), &emptyRows))
121		assert.Empty(t, emptyRows)
122	}

26	// executeCapturingStub is a FizeauService that records Execute calls and fails
27	// loudly if ResolveRoute is invoked — enforcing the CONTRACT-003 boundary
28	// that execution paths must not pre-resolve routes.
29	type executeCapturingStub struct {
30		mu            sync.Mutex
31		executeCalled bool
32		lastReq       agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
33		executionReq  agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
34		executionSeen bool
35		executeFn     func(agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error)
36		listModels    []agentlib.ModelInfo
37		listProfiles  []agentlib.ProfileInfo
38	}
39	
40	func (s *executeCapturingStub) Execute(_ context.Context, req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
41		s.mu.Lock()
42		s.executeCalled = true
43		s.lastReq = req
44		if req.Role == "implementer" {
45			s.executionReq = req
46			s.executionSeen = true
47		}
48		s.mu.Unlock()
49		if s.executeFn != nil {
50			return s.executeFn(req)
51		}
52		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
53		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
54		close(ch)
55		return ch, nil
56	}
57	
58	func (s *executeCapturingStub) ResolveRoute(_ context.Context, _ agentlib.RouteRequest) (*agentlib.RouteDecision, error) {
59		return nil, fmt.Errorf("routinglint: ResolveRoute called in execution path — violates CONTRACT-003 / ddx-da19756a")
60	}
61	
62	func (s *executeCapturingStub) TailSessionLog(_ context.Context, _ string) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
63		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent)
64		close(ch)
65		return ch, nil
66	}

68		return []agentlib.HarnessInfo{{Name: "claude", Available: true}, {Name: "agent", Available: true}}, nil
69	}

72	}

75	}

107	// installExecuteCapturingStub injects stub as the service used by
108	// RunWithConfigViaService and registers cleanup. Returns the stub so callers
109	// can inspect captured fields after the run.
110	func installExecuteCapturingStub(t *testing.T) *executeCapturingStub {
111		t.Helper()
112		stub := &executeCapturingStub{}
113		agent.SetServiceRunFactory(func(_ string) (agentlib.FizeauService, error) {
114			return stub, nil
115		})
116		t.Cleanup(func() { agent.SetServiceRunFactory(nil) })
117		return stub
118	}

144	// TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute (AC): ddx work must not call ResolveRoute
145	// in the execution path. The stub returns an error from ResolveRoute so any
146	// call to it would surface as a command failure carrying "ResolveRoute called".
147	// MinPower/MaxPower passthrough to ServiceExecuteRequest is proved statically
148	// by the singleTierAttempt loopOverrides construction and by the internal
149	// agent package's passthrough_envelope_test.go.
150	func TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute(t *testing.T) {
151		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
152	
153		stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
154		_ = stub // stub.ResolveRoute returns error; any call would propagate up
155	
156		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
157	
158		// Seed one ready bead so the loop has something to claim.
159		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
160		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
161		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
162			ID:    "ddx-passthrough-power-test",
163			Title: "passthrough power test bead",
164		}))
165	
166		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
167		_, err := executeCommand(root, "work",
168			"--once",
169			"--harness", "claude",
170			"--model", "claude-opus-4-7",
171			"--min-power", "40",
172			"--max-power", "90",
173		)
174		// If ResolveRoute were called, the stub returns an error containing
175		// "ResolveRoute called in execution path". Verify it didn't happen.
176		if err != nil {
177			require.NotContains(t, err.Error(), "ResolveRoute called in execution path",
178				"ddx work must not call ResolveRoute (CONTRACT-003 / ddx-da19756a)")
179		}
180		// Other errors (git not initialized, etc.) are expected and acceptable —
181		// the test's only concern is that ResolveRoute was not invoked.
182	}

185	// --harness flag sends an empty Harness to the agent service, allowing the
186	// service's routing engine to auto-select a provider from configured endpoints.
187	func TestWorkPassesEmptyHarnessToService(t *testing.T) {
188		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
189

191	
192		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)

199		}))
200	
201		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
202		_, _ = executeCommand(root, "work", "--once")
203	
204		stub.mu.Lock()
205		executeCalled := stub.executeCalled
206		lastReq := stub.lastReq
207		stub.mu.Unlock()

209		if !executeCalled {
210			t.Skip("Execute not called — bead may have been short-circuited before service dispatch")
211		}
212		assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Harness,
213			"ddx work with no --harness must send empty Harness to service for engine auto-selection")
214	}

258	func TestDDxWork_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook(t *testing.T) {
259		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
260	
261		var mu sync.Mutex
262		modes := make([]string, 0, 4)
263		var intakeReq agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
264		stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
265		stub.listProfiles = []agentlib.ProfileInfo{
266			{Name: "cheap", MinPower: 5, MaxPower: 5},
267			{Name: "smart", MinPower: 9, MaxPower: 10},
268		}
269		stub.listModels = []agentlib.ModelInfo{
270			{ID: "cheap-model", Power: 5, Available: true, AutoRoutable: true},
271			{ID: "smart-model", Power: 9, Available: true, AutoRoutable: true},
272		}
273		stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
274			mode := "execute"
275			switch {
276			case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: intake"):
277				mode = "intake"
278			case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: lint"):
279				mode = "lint"
280			case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: triage"):
281				mode = "triage"
282			}
283			mu.Lock()
284			modes = append(modes, mode)
285			if mode == "intake" {
286				intakeReq = req
287			}
288			mu.Unlock()
289	
290			switch mode {
291			case "intake":
292				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
293				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"atomic\",\"confidence\":0.99,\"reasoning\":\"single-slice\"}"}`)}
294				close(ch)
295				return ch, nil
296			case "lint":
297				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
298				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"score\":9,\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"suggested_fixes\":[],\"waivers_applied\":[]}"}`)}
299				close(ch)
300				return ch, nil
301			case "triage":
302				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
303				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"already_satisfied\",\"recommended_action\":\"close_already_satisfied\",\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"suggested_amendments\":[],\"suggested_followup_beads\":[]}"}`)}
304				close(ch)
305				return ch, nil
306			default:
307				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
308				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
309				close(ch)
310				return ch, nil
311			}
312		}
313	
314		dir := setupWorkIntakeFixture(t)
315		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
316		_, _ = executeCommand(root, "work", "--once", "--max-power", "8")
317	
318		mu.Lock()
319		got := append([]string(nil), modes...)
320		gotIntakeReq := intakeReq
321		mu.Unlock()
322		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(got), 1, "work must invoke the intake hook")
323		assert.Equal(t, "intake", got[0], "plain ddx work must run pre-claim intake before claim")
324		assert.Equal(t, "smart", gotIntakeReq.Profile)
325		assert.Empty(t, gotIntakeReq.Harness)
326		assert.Empty(t, gotIntakeReq.Provider)
327		assert.Empty(t, gotIntakeReq.Model)
328		assert.Empty(t, gotIntakeReq.ModelRef)
329		assert.Zero(t, gotIntakeReq.MinPower)
330		assert.Zero(t, gotIntakeReq.MaxPower, "intake dispatch must not inherit implementation power bounds")
331		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(got), 2, "work must continue past intake to later execution stages")
332	}
